title: ImplementAndGateOutput
description: Workflow output for implement-and-gate. `gates_green` carries the final all-green battery record; `gates_exhausted` carries the LAST round's record — the failing gate's tail-bounded captured output included — after fix_cap fix rounds: cap exhaustion is a finding surfaced to the operator, never an error crash. The workspace is intentionally NOT torn down on either terminus (review inspects success; the operator inspects failure). The embedded report is shaped by the sibling implementation-report.schema.json (kept loose here so this file stays self-contained for the packager).

outcome.description: gates_green = every gate exited 0 on the final round; gates_exhausted = fix_cap fix rounds were spent and a gate is still red.

implementation_report.description: The implementer's LAST full replacement report, shaped by schemas/implementation-report.schema.json. It never certifies gates — the gate_record beside it is the verdict.

command.description: The exact command whose OWN exit status judged the gate.

exit_status.description: The command's own exit status — the durable verdict (128+signal when signal-killed).

output_tail.description: Tail-bounded captured output. Present only on the failing entry of a gates_exhausted record — the durable record the operator (and any future fix round) reads, never a paraphrase.

gate_record.description: The final round's battery record in gate order. On gates_green: every declared gate, all exit 0. On gates_exhausted: the gates run that round, the red one last.

rounds.description: Gate-battery rounds executed = implementer rounds spent (1 initial + one per fix round).

workspace_path.description: The isolated workspace holding the diff. Deliberately left in place on both termini.
